name: Layer by Layer (Classic)
description: The classic layer-by-layer method that most cubers learn as their very first approach to solving the Rubik's Cube. LBL breaks the solve into 7 clearly defined steps, solving the cube one layer at a time from bottom to top. Each step uses only 1-2 short algorithms, making it extremely accessible. While it shares DNA with the beginner's method, this formulation emphasizes the full 7-step breakdown with explicit cross, corners, and edges stages for each layer.

steps.name: First Layer Cross
steps.description: Form a plus sign (cross) on the first layer (usually white), ensuring each edge piece matches the center color of its adjacent face. This establishes the foundation of the solve.
tips: Start by finding the white edges in the scramble
The daisy method (making a yellow-face cross first) can simplify this
Make sure each edge matches the side center color, not just the white face

steps.name: First Layer Corners
steps.description: Insert the 4 corner pieces of the first layer into their correct positions. Each corner is placed by positioning it above its target slot and using a short trigger algorithm.
tips: Use R U R' U' (the sexy move) repeatedly to cycle corners
Position each corner directly above its target slot
May need up to 5 repetitions of the trigger per corner

steps.name: Second Layer Edges
steps.description: Insert the 4 edge pieces of the middle layer into their correct positions. Two mirror algorithms handle inserting edges to the left or right.
tips: Only 2 algorithms needed: one for left insert, one for right insert
Find edges in the top layer that do not have yellow on them
If an edge is stuck flipped in the middle layer, insert any edge to bump it out

steps.name: Last Layer Cross
steps.description: Form a cross on the last layer (yellow face) by orienting the 4 edge pieces so yellow faces up. This step does not worry about edge positions, only orientation.
tips: F R U R' U' F' is the key algorithm
Apply based on the current pattern: dot, L-shape, or line
Dot requires the algorithm 3 times, L-shape twice, line once

steps.name: Last Layer Cross Permutation
steps.description: Move the yellow cross edges to their correct positions so each edge matches the center color of its adjacent face. The yellow cross is now correctly aligned.
tips: Look for two adjacent edges that are already correct
R U R' U R U2 R' U cycle algorithm
May need to apply the algorithm 1-2 times

steps.name: Last Layer Corners Position
steps.description: Move the last layer corners to their correct positions (the correct corner in each slot), without worrying about their orientation. Find one correctly positioned corner and cycle the others.
tips: Look for a corner where all 3 colors match the surrounding centers
Use U R U' L' U R' U' L to cycle 3 corners
If no corner is correct, apply the algorithm once and then recheck

steps.name: Last Layer Corners Orientation
steps.description: Orient the last layer corners so that yellow faces up on all of them, completing the cube. Each corner is twisted in place using a repeated trigger without disturbing the rest of the solve.
tips: Use R' D' R D repeated 2 or 4 times per corner
Do NOT turn the top layer between corners; only turn the bottom layer to bring the next corner into position
The cube will look scrambled during this step, but trust the process

pros: Very easy to understand with clear visual progress at each step
Perfect first method for absolute beginners
Abundant tutorials in every format and language
cons: Slow solve times even when well-practiced
No natural progression path toward speed
Many separate steps make the solve feel lengthy
Layer restriction prevents efficient piece placement
creator: David Singmaster
